description: LLMSafeSpaces operational health: availability, lifecycle, saturation, noisy neighbors, controller, security, metering integrity. Every panel has a critical threshold and drives a specific operator action.

panels.title: Platform Availability
panels.description: The single most important question: is the platform serving users? Availability = 1 - 5xx/(total - 4xx). 4xx are excluded because they are client errors, not server failures. Critical threshold: 99%.

panels.title: Availability (5xx-free of server-handled requests)
panels.description: Availability = 1 - (5xx errors / (total - 4xx)). 4xx are excluded from the denominator because they are client-side failures (bad auth, not found, quota). Below 99% = CRITICAL. Alert fires at <99% for 3 of 5 data points. Action: page on-call, check dependency status, consider rollback.

panels.title: Active Workspaces
panels.description: Current count of workspaces in the Active phase. Use this to gauge platform load and correlate with resource panels below. A sudden drop indicates mass workspace failures — check Phase Transitions.

panels.title: Suspended Workspaces (PVC retained)
panels.description: Workspaces in Suspended phase. Each retains a PVC consuming storage. High count = storage waste. Action: evict long-idle suspended workspaces or implement idle-eviction policy.

panels.title: Safe Mode Workspaces
panels.description: Workspaces in Safe Mode (repeated failure, awaiting manual intervention). Any non-zero value requires operator action — affected workspaces will NOT auto-recover. Action: investigate the failing workspace, fix root cause, manually clear safe mode.

panels.title: Legacy Plaintext API Keys
panels.description: API keys still using pre-DEK plaintext storage. Target is 0. Any non-zero value is a security risk — action: force key rotation for affected users.

panels.title: Request Composition (2xx / 4xx / 5xx)
panels.description: Stacked request rate by response class. Green (2xx) = successful traffic. Yellow (4xx) = client errors (auth failures, bad input, quota). Red (5xx) = server faults. A growing red wedge is the primary signal of platform degradation. Use the Error Status Breakdown panel to see specific 4xx/5xx codes.

panels.title: Error Status Breakdown (by HTTP code)
panels.description: Non-2xx request rate broken down by specific status code. Stories: 401 spike = expired JWT wave or brute-force. 403 = authorization misconfiguration. 404 = routing bug or stale client. 429 = quota enforcement active (expected under load). 500/502/503 = server faults requiring investigation. Red line = 1 req/s (any non-2xx at this rate is notable).

panels.title: API Latency p95 / p99
panels.description: p95 and p99 response latency (p50 dropped — only the tail is actionable). Red line at 2s: above this, users experience noticeable delays. Sustained p99 above 5s likely reflects slow workspace proxy calls to agentd. Action: scale API replicas, check DB/Redis latency, investigate slow agentd endpoints.

panels.title: Workspace Lifecycle
panels.description: How fast do workspaces launch, and are any stuck in failure loops? Create duration is the primary user-facing UX metric. Resume duration is ~22s post-optimization (n=5 home-cluster benchmark, 2026-06-24); the original ~3s design target was unvalidated and is currently aspirational. Restart loops indicate systemic problems.

panels.title: Workspace Create Duration p95 / p99
panels.description: Wall-clock time from creation request to Active phase. p50 is typically 30-90s (image pull + init container). Red line at 120s: users waiting >2min for a workspace will perceive the platform as broken. Action: pre-pull runtime images onto nodes, add nodes, investigate slow init containers (check Agentd Startup Gate).

panels.title: Workspace Resume Duration p95 / p99
panels.description: Time from resume request to Active (pod deleted, PVC retained, re-created). Measured baseline post-optimization is ~22s (n=5 home-cluster benchmark, 2026-06-24); the original ~3s design target was unvalidated and is currently aspirational. Red line at 10s: useful for detecting acute regressions (slow PVC attach, node pressure, image pull miss); current measured baseline already exceeds this threshold pending PVC-attach or warm-pool work. Action when crossing line: check node health, ensure runtime images are cached on resume-target nodes.

panels.title: Workspace Failure & Restart Rate
panels.description: Rate of workspaces entering Failed or re-entering Creating (restart loops). Non-zero is CRITICAL. Action: investigate why workspaces are failing — check Recovery Attempts vs Success panel, examine controller logs for phase handler errors. reason label on failed_total identifies OOM, crash, eviction, etc.

panels.title: Resource Saturation & Dependencies
panels.description: Is the platform running out of capacity? DB pool exhaustion, slow queries, Redis latency, SSE reliability, and dependency up/down. Relay and OIDC health are reflected in traffic metrics (inference errors, auth failures).

panels.title: DB Connection Pool Utilization
panels.description: Active DB connections as % of pool max. Red line at 80%: above this, the API is approaching pool exhaustion — new requests will queue or fail. Action: increase maxOpenConns, scale PostgreSQL, or investigate long-running queries holding connections.

panels.title: DB Query Latency p95 + Errors
panels.description: p95 query latency by operation (select/insert/update/delete) and error rate. Red line at 50ms: slow queries indicate missing indexes, lock contention, or Postgres CPU pressure. Error lines >0 require investigation (timeout, deadlock, connection). Action: EXPLAIN ANALYZE slow queries, check pg_stat_activity for locks.

panels.title: Redis Latency p95 + Errors
panels.description: p95 Redis command latency by command type and error rate. Red line at 5ms: Redis should be sub-millisecond. Elevated latency indicates Redis CPU saturation, network issues, or large value serialization. Action: check Redis INFO, scale Redis, investigate large cached values.

panels.title: SSE Dropped Events (real-time update reliability)
panels.description: Rate of SSE events dropped because subscriber channel buffer was full. Red line at 1/s: any dropped events mean users are missing real-time updates (phase changes, session status, chat streaming). They must wait for next poll to catch up. Action: increase SSE broker buffer size, investigate slow browser clients, check if burst events (bulk phase changes) exceed buffer.

panels.title: Dependency Status (postgres, redis)
panels.description: Health status of PostgreSQL and Redis (1=up, 0=down). Updated on each /readyz call. Relay health is reflected in Inference metrics on the Billing dashboard; OIDC/auth health is reflected in the Auth Failure Ratio panel. Any dependency showing 0 = the platform is degraded or down. Action: restart the failed dependency, check network connectivity.

panels.title: Noisy Neighbor Detection — by Workspace
panels.description: Top-10 workspaces by CPU, memory, and disk. Pinpoints the specific workspace pod consuming disproportionate resources. Each bar gauge shows the top-10 consumers at the current instant. See the by-User row below for per-tenant aggregates.

panels.title: Top-10 Workspaces by CPU
panels.description: Top-10 workspace pods by CPU consumption (cores). Red threshold at 4 cores (default burst limit = 4x 500m request). Workspaces above this are CPU-bound and may need resource limit increases or investigation for runaway processes. Action: terminate or throttle the workspace, contact the user.

panels.title: Top-10 Workspaces by Memory
panels.description: Top-10 workspace pods by cgroup memory usage. Red threshold at 2Gi (default burst limit = 4x 512Mi request). Workspaces approaching their memory limit risk OOM kills. Action: increase memory limit for legitimate use, terminate workspaces with memory leaks.

panels.title: Top-10 Workspaces by Disk % of PVC
panels.description: Top-10 workspaces by disk usage as % of allocated PVC. Red threshold at 95%: workspaces near their PVC limit risk filling up and crashing the agent (no space for opencode.db, auth.json, etc.). Action: alert user, resize PVC, or clean up large files.

panels.title: Top-10 Users by Active Compute (parallel workspaces)
panels.description: Top-10 users by active compute rate. A value of 1.0 = one workspace continuously active. Values above 1.0 = user has multiple concurrent workspaces. Red threshold at 10: a single user with 10+ active workspaces may be abusing the platform. Action: enforce per-user workspace quota, contact user.

panels.title: Noisy Neighbor Detection — by User
panels.description: Top-10 users by aggregated workspace CPU, memory, and disk, plus concurrent active compute. A user with high values across multiple panels has both heavy single-workspace usage and parallel workspaces. Action: enforce per-user quota, contact user, or terminate workspaces.

panels.title: Top-10 Users by CPU
panels.description: Top-10 users by aggregated CPU across all their workspaces (cores). Red threshold at 16 cores = 4 workspaces continuously bursting at the per-workspace red of 4 cores each. A user above this is using more compute than the platform's average tenant by a wide margin. Action: enforce per-user CPU quota, contact user.

panels.title: Top-10 Users by Memory
panels.description: Top-10 users by aggregated memory across all their workspaces (bytes). Red threshold at 8 GiB = 4 workspaces continuously near the per-workspace red of 2 GiB each. Users approaching this likely have many parallel workspaces or memory leaks. Action: enforce per-user memory quota, investigate, contact user.

panels.title: Top-10 Users by Disk % of PVC
panels.description: Top-10 users by aggregated disk usage as % of their total allocated PVC space across all workspaces. Red threshold at 95%: a user near their aggregate PVC limit risks workspace agent crashes (no space for opencode.db, auth.json, etc.). Action: alert user, audit large files, resize PVCs, or enforce per-user disk quota.

panels.title: Controller & Recovery
panels.description: Controller reconcile loop health and workspace recovery effectiveness. A healthy controller shows near-zero errors and high recovery success rates.

panels.title: Reconciliation Duration p99
panels.description: p99 duration of the controller reconcile loop per resource type. Red line at 2s: this is the per-iteration cost, not end-to-end creation time. Sustained high values indicate slow K8s API calls, large workspace backlog, or controller CPU starvation. Action: check controller CPU/memory limits, check K8s API server latency.

panels.title: Reconciliation Errors
panels.description: Error rate in the controller reconcile loop by resource and error type. Any non-zero rate is critical — the controller relies on successful reconciles to progress workspace state. get_failed = cannot fetch workspace from API server. phase_handler = error in creating/active/terminating handler. Action: check controller logs, check K8s API server health.

panels.title: Recovery Success Ratio (by failure class)
panels.description: Fraction of recovery attempts that successfully returned to Active, per failure class. Red line at 75%: below this, the system cannot reliably self-heal. Classes: infrastructure (node/pod eviction), resource (OOM/CPU limit), process (app crash), configuration (bad user spec). A low ratio for a class indicates a systemic issue. Action: investigate why that failure class is not recovering — may need image fixes, resource limit changes, or configuration validation.

panels.title: Security & Auth
panels.description: Authentication health and credential rotation reliability. Auth failure ratio and 4xx error types reveal brute-force attempts, expired credential waves, and quota enforcement activity.

panels.title: Auth Failure Ratio + Reasons
panels.description: Top line: auth failure ratio (% of all auth attempts that failed). Red threshold at 15%: sustained high failure rates indicate brute-force attack or widespread credential expiry. Bottom lines: raw failure rate by reason (invalid_token, expired_token, wrong_password, revoked_token, missing_token). Action: check for brute-force patterns, verify JWT expiry config, investigate revoked_token spikes (key rotation issues).

panels.title: Client Error Types (401 / 403 / 404 / 429)
panels.description: Actionable breakdown of 4xx error codes. 401 Unauthorized = auth failures (expired JWT, invalid API key). 403 Forbidden = authorization failures (wrong scope, locked account). 404 Not Found = routing bugs or stale client references. 429 Too Many Requests = quota enforcement active (expected under load, confirms limits work). Action: 401 spike = check JWT config or brute-force. 403 spike = RBAC misconfiguration. 404 spike = routing bug. 429 = quota working as designed.

panels.title: Agent Reload Failure Ratio (credential rotation health)
panels.description: Fraction of agent reload operations that failed. Reloads happen when user credentials are rotated. Red threshold at 10%: a high failure rate means credential rotations are failing — users will NOT receive updated credentials in their running workspaces. Action: check agentd logs for reload errors, verify opencode dispose/restart mechanism.

panels.title: Metering Pipeline Integrity
panels.description: Is billing data trustworthy? These panels verify the metering pipeline is recording, batching, and exporting usage events correctly. Non-zero dropped events or growing DLQ means billing data is incomplete — costs shown on the Billing dashboard may be understated.

panels.title: Metering Events: Recorded vs Failed vs Dropped
panels.description: Pipeline throughput by outcome. Recorded (green) = events successfully written to usage_events table. Failed (yellow) = batch write failed, event went to DLQ. Dropped (red) = buffer full or DLQ write also failed — data is LOST. Any dropped events are critical: billing data is incomplete. Action: check PostgreSQL connectivity, check DLQ depth, verify batch write latency.

panels.title: DLQ Depth (unresolved entries)
panels.description: Current dead-letter queue size. Non-zero entries are events that failed batch write and are awaiting reprocessing by the DLQ reaper. Red threshold at 1: any unresolved DLQ entries mean the reaper is failing. Action: check DLQ reaper logs, verify Postgres is reachable, manually reprocess via admin API.

panels.title: DLQ Dead Entries (exhausted retries)
panels.description: DLQ entries that exhausted all 5 retry attempts. These events are permanently lost from billing data. Non-zero requires manual intervention via admin API to reprocess or discard. Action: investigate why events repeatedly failed, fix root cause, manually reprocess if possible.

panels.title: Billing Export Lag
panels.description: Seconds since last successful billing export. Red threshold at 900s (15min): rising lag indicates the export goroutine is failing. Billing data forwarded to external billing providers is stale. Action: check export goroutine logs, verify external billing provider connectivity.

title: LLMSafeSpaces - Operational
